body.title: [gated] GATED: Organizing-question cascade — name each level's organizing-question set
body.description:
**GATED — do not sprint until debt-clearing done (aider-port batch + repo cleanup) and Akien reopens. Vision still crystallizing; full per-level decomposition + audit chain deferred to gate-open.**

**Problem / shape:** Every build level (design, architecture, ticket, workflow, file, symbol) has a finite SET OF ORGANIZING QUESTIONS = that level's rubric AND its resolution-loop checklist. Intention descends by filtering through each level's set; code is the instantiation of the answered questions. Because descent is deterministically pinned top-down, conflict has no unpinned surface to form on. See D-organizing-question-cascade-2026-07-07 for the full narrative, resolution-loop (4 outcomes: extant-pattern / flag-missing-work / escalate-to-meta-question / spawn-Watch-For), and cortical-columns grounding.

**First concrete deliverable when gate opens:** the cross-level map — for each level, name its finite organizing-question set. Pre-fill levels that already compile (code -> does-it-pass; model-select -> cost/capability argmin; build -> evaluator judge panel). Real work = the empty rows: DESIGN and ARCHITECTURE. Scope boundary: organizing questions ONLY (the few that structure the level), NOT an exhaustive enumeration of every question needed to replace the LLM.

**Completion test (design level, when built):** a reviewer can adjudicate a hand-picked pair AND a NEAR-TIE pair of competing designs using only the named set. If the set can't discriminate the near-tie, the missing organizing question has been found (that failure is the informative outcome).

**Unifies existing tickets (do not duplicate):** T-substrate-experiment-cascade (conductor that walks levels before escalating), T-experiment-primitive (EPIC substrate), T-inference-outcome-learner (observe->discover), and the existing repo-pattern-collection ticket that populates design_patterns/ (the extant-pattern library outcome-1 queries).

**Affected files:** TBD at gate-open (spec/store artifacts under devlab/runtime/memory/; likely touches evaluator + design_patterns store). No code changes while gated.

**Design rules:** proof-on-close; organizing-questions-only scope boundary; do not freeze the vision by over-decomposing while molten.

**Test plan:** the near-tie adjudication test above; audit-design decidability on the named sets.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.gate: debt-clearing complete (aider-port batch + repo cleanup) AND Akien reopens
